[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.

The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Meryō]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Meryō]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Meryō-qq]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Meryō-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Meryō-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:qq-Meryō-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
[[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:Meryō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Meryō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Meryō--> [[Category:馬寮]][[ja:馬寮]] {{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781年}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|執権}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

馬寮
