馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:馬寮 up to rev 100867721
revision.text:
{{複数の問題|出典の明記=2018年6月}} '''馬寮'''（めりょう/うまのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]における[[官司]]の一つ。[[唐名]]では'''[[典厩]]'''（てんきゅう）。'''左馬寮'''（さめりょう/さまりょう）と'''右馬寮'''（うめりょう/うまりょう）に分かれていた<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=馬文化ひだか：馬用語検索：馬寮（めりょう）|date=2021-10-27}}</ref>
== 概要 == [[令制国|諸国]]の牧（御牧・官牧）から貢上された[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]保有の[[馬]]の[[飼育]]・[[調教]]にあたった官職である<ref name=":0" />。諸国から集められた馬は馬寮直轄の[[厩舎]]や牧（寮牧・近都牧）で飼養したり、[[畿内]]及び周辺諸国に命じて飼養させた。また、後には[[勅旨牧]]の経営も監督した。そして軍事や儀式において必要なときに牽進させて必要部署に供給した。
馬寮の官人は[[武官]]とされて帯剣を許された。後には[[検非違使]]を補助して都の治安維持の業務にあたる事もあった。後に頭以下の官職に[[武士]]が任官されるのもこうした[[警察]]的な要素があったからとも考えられる。[[官人#雑任|伴部]]として実際に馬の飼育にあたる[[馬部]]（めぶ）がおり、飼育を担当する[[飼戸]]（しこ）を傘下においてこれを統率した。
== 沿革 == === 奈良時代 === [[大宝律令]]で左馬寮・右馬寮が設置された。当初は頭（左馬頭・右馬頭）を長としていたが、[[711年]]に馬の軍事的な重要性から[[従五位下]]ながら[[皇族]]である[[葛木王]]（後の[[橘諸兄]]）が[[令外官]]である'''[[馬寮監]]'''（めりょうげん）に任じられて左右馬寮を統括した。馬寮監は非常設で後に左右それぞれに設置されている。
[[765年]]の[[近衛府]]設置と同時に宮中の[[厩]]を扱う'''[[内厩寮]]'''（ないきゅうりょう）が分離されて基本的に[[近衛府]]の官人がこれを兼ね、残った左右の馬寮も[[781年]]に'''[[主馬寮]]'''（しゅめりょう）に統合されて長である'''主馬頭'''（しゅめのかみ）には[[従四位下]]である[[伊勢老人]]が[[任官|任じられた]]。
=== 平安時代 === [[808年]]には主馬寮を左馬寮、内厩寮を右馬寮と改名して律令制当初の呼称が復活し、[[兵部省]]に属していた[[兵馬司]]を吸収した。その後、馬寮監も'''馬寮御監'''（めりょうごげん/みかん）として復活するが、その職掌は左右馬頭からあげられた儀式の際の馬について報告を[[天皇]]に奏上するだけに留まり、実際には名義のみを継承したものである。ただし、[[近衛大将|左右近衛大将]]との兼務が慣例とされたためにその官位相当は[[従三位]]と大幅に上昇しており、馬寮では名目上の最高職であった。
[[平安時代]]後期以後は実質上の最高職である左右馬頭に[[河内源氏]]、[[伊勢平氏]]の著名な武者が相次いで任じられたことから馬寮の職は[[武士]]の憧れの[[官職]]の一つとされた。[[地下人|地下]]の武士でも、大允・少允であれば[[成功 (任官)|成功]]で獲得することが可能であったために志望者が殺到し[[久安]]4年（[[1148年]]）に大允・少允の定員は計20名に増やされたが、平安時代末期にはその数倍の者が任じられていたという（『[[官職秘抄]]』）。
=== 鎌倉時代 === 三代将軍の[[源実朝]]は左馬寮御監に叙任された。
[[執権]]や[[連署]]の多くは、頭、権頭、助に任ぜられた。
=== 室町時代 === [[足利義満]]は右馬寮御監に叙任された。以後、足利将軍の歴代では、[[足利義持]]、[[足利義教]]、[[足利義政]]、[[足利義尚]]らが右馬寮御監に任ぜられた。
左馬頭は、[[足利直義]]の任官を嘉例とし、将軍家や鎌倉公方・古河公方の当主・子弟が就任する官職と看做された。[[足利将軍一覧|室町将軍]]や[[鎌倉公方]]・[[古河公方]]の歴代以外には、[[足利義嗣]]{{ #tag:ref|義満の子、義持の異母弟。| group=注釈}}、[[足利義視]]{{ #tag:ref|義教の子、義政の異母弟、義稙の父。| group=注釈}}、[[足利義維]]{{ #tag:ref|義澄の子、義稙の養子、義晴の異母兄弟、義栄の父。| group=注釈}}が就任している。 また、[[足利義輝]]が謀殺された[[永禄の変]]の後には、後継と目された弟の[[足利義昭]]と従弟の[[足利義栄]]との両名を、朝廷は何れも左馬頭に立て続けに任じて事態の推移を見守ったこともあった。
[[佐竹氏]]や[[岩松氏]]、[[里見氏#安房里見氏|安房里見氏]]などのように、関東には左馬頭を自称する一族がいた。
[[細川氏#典厩家|細川典厩家]]が代々右馬頭・右馬助に叙任されたほか、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[毛利元就]]や[[毛利輝元]]も右馬頭に叙任されている。
=== 江戸時代 === [[徳川家康]]は左馬寮御監に、[[徳川家光]]は右馬寮御監に叙任された。以後、右馬寮御監は徳川将軍が兼帯する官職として、最後の[[徳川慶喜]]まで慣例となった。
[[徳川綱重]]は左馬頭に、[[徳川綱吉]]は右馬頭に叙任された。
[[足利氏]]（鎌倉公方・古河公方）の後裔である[[喜連川氏]]は、左馬頭を[[仮名 (通称)|自称]]することが黙認されており、歴代で数名が称している。
== 役職 == * 御監（[[従三位]]相当・[[令外官]]※）左右各一名 :左馬寮御監は[[近衛大将|左近衛大将]]、右馬寮御監は[[近衛大将|右近衛大将]]の兼務。 * 頭（[[従五位|従五位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 助（[[正六位|正六位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 大允（[[正七位|正七位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 大属（[[従八位|従八位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 少属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 馬医（従八位上相当）左右各二名
* 史生 左右各二名（後四名） * 馬部 左右各六十名 * 使部 左右各二十名 * 直丁 左右各二名 * 飼丁
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * {{Kotobank}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:めりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:馬に関する文化]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[馬寮]] to [[Meryō]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{複数の問題|出典の明記=2018年6月}} '''馬寮'''（めりょう/うまのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]における[[官司]]の一つ。[[唐名]]では'''[[典厩]]'''（てんきゅう）。'''左馬寮'''（さめりょう/さまりょう）と'''右馬寮'''（うめりょう/うまりょう）に分かれていた<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=馬文化ひだか：馬用語検索：馬寮（めりょう）|date=2021-10-27}}</ref>
== 概要 == [[令制国|諸国]]の牧（御牧・官牧）から貢上された[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]保有の[[馬]]の[[飼育]]・[[調教]]にあたった官職である<ref name=":0" />。諸国から集められた馬は馬寮直轄の[[厩舎]]や牧（寮牧・近都牧）で飼養したり、[[畿内]]及び周辺諸国に命じて飼養させた。また、後には[[勅旨牧]]の経営も監督した。そして軍事や儀式において必要なときに牽進させて必要部署に供給した。
馬寮の官人は[[武官]]とされて帯剣を許された。後には[[検非違使]]を補助して都の治安維持の業務にあたる事もあった。後に頭以下の官職に[[武士]]が任官されるのもこうした[[警察]]的な要素があったからとも考えられる。[[官人#雑任|伴部]]として実際に馬の飼育にあたる[[馬部]]（めぶ）がおり、飼育を担当する[[飼戸]]（しこ）を傘下においてこれを統率した。
== 沿革 == === 奈良時代 === [[大宝律令]]で左馬寮・右馬寮が設置された。当初は頭（左馬頭・右馬頭）を長としていたが、[[711年]]に馬の軍事的な重要性から[[従五位下]]ながら[[皇族]]である[[葛木王]]（後の[[橘諸兄]]）が[[令外官]]である'''[[馬寮監]]'''（めりょうげん）に任じられて左右馬寮を統括した。馬寮監は非常設で後に左右それぞれに設置されている。
[[765年]]の[[近衛府]]設置と同時に宮中の[[厩]]を扱う'''[[内厩寮]]'''（ないきゅうりょう）が分離されて基本的に[[近衛府]]の官人がこれを兼ね、残った左右の馬寮も[[781年]]に'''[[主馬寮]]'''（しゅめりょう）に統合されて長である'''主馬頭'''（しゅめのかみ）には[[従四位下]]である[[伊勢老人]]が[[任官|任じられた]]。
=== 平安時代 === [[808年]]には主馬寮を左馬寮、内厩寮を右馬寮と改名して律令制当初の呼称が復活し、[[兵部省]]に属していた[[兵馬司]]を吸収した。その後、馬寮監も'''馬寮御監'''（めりょうごげん/みかん）として復活するが、その職掌は左右馬頭からあげられた儀式の際の馬について報告を[[天皇]]に奏上するだけに留まり、実際には名義のみを継承したものである。ただし、[[近衛大将|左右近衛大将]]との兼務が慣例とされたためにその官位相当は[[従三位]]と大幅に上昇しており、馬寮では名目上の最高職であった。
[[平安時代]]後期以後は実質上の最高職である左右馬頭に[[河内源氏]]、[[伊勢平氏]]の著名な武者が相次いで任じられたことから馬寮の職は[[武士]]の憧れの[[官職]]の一つとされた。[[地下人|地下]]の武士でも、大允・少允であれば[[成功 (任官)|成功]]で獲得することが可能であったために志望者が殺到し[[久安]]4年（[[1148年]]）に大允・少允の定員は計20名に増やされたが、平安時代末期にはその数倍の者が任じられていたという（『[[官職秘抄]]』）。
=== 鎌倉時代 === 三代将軍の[[源実朝]]は左馬寮御監に叙任された。
[[執権]]や[[連署]]の多くは、頭、権頭、助に任ぜられた。
=== 室町時代 === [[足利義満]]は右馬寮御監に叙任された。以後、足利将軍の歴代では、[[足利義持]]、[[足利義教]]、[[足利義政]]、[[足利義尚]]らが右馬寮御監に任ぜられた。
左馬頭は、[[足利直義]]の任官を嘉例とし、将軍家や鎌倉公方・古河公方の当主・子弟が就任する官職と看做された。[[足利将軍一覧|室町将軍]]や[[鎌倉公方]]・[[古河公方]]の歴代以外には、[[足利義嗣]]{{ #tag:ref|義満の子、義持の異母弟。| group=注釈}}、[[足利義視]]{{ #tag:ref|義教の子、義政の異母弟、義稙の父。| group=注釈}}、[[足利義維]]{{ #tag:ref|義澄の子、義稙の養子、義晴の異母兄弟、義栄の父。| group=注釈}}が就任している。 また、[[足利義輝]]が謀殺された[[永禄の変]]の後には、後継と目された弟の[[足利義昭]]と従弟の[[足利義栄]]との両名を、朝廷は何れも左馬頭に立て続けに任じて事態の推移を見守ったこともあった。
[[佐竹氏]]や[[岩松氏]]、[[里見氏#安房里見氏|安房里見氏]]などのように、関東には左馬頭を自称する一族がいた。
[[細川氏#典厩家|細川典厩家]]が代々右馬頭・右馬助に叙任されたほか、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[毛利元就]]や[[毛利輝元]]も右馬頭に叙任されている。
=== 江戸時代 === [[徳川家康]]は左馬寮御監に、[[徳川家光]]は右馬寮御監に叙任された。以後、右馬寮御監は徳川将軍が兼帯する官職として、最後の[[徳川慶喜]]まで慣例となった。
[[徳川綱重]]は左馬頭に、[[徳川綱吉]]は右馬頭に叙任された。
[[足利氏]]（鎌倉公方・古河公方）の後裔である[[喜連川氏]]は、左馬頭を[[仮名 (通称)|自称]]することが黙認されており、歴代で数名が称している。
== 役職 == * 御監（[[従三位]]相当・[[令外官]]※）左右各一名 :左馬寮御監は[[近衛大将|左近衛大将]]、右馬寮御監は[[近衛大将|右近衛大将]]の兼務。 * 頭（[[従五位|従五位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 助（[[正六位|正六位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 大允（[[正七位|正七位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 大属（[[従八位|従八位上]]相当）左右各一名 * 少属（[[従八位|従八位下]]相当）左右各一名 * 馬医（従八位上相当）左右各二名
* 史生 左右各二名（後四名） * 馬部 左右各六十名 * 使部 左右各二十名 * 直丁 左右各二名 * 飼丁
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * {{Kotobank}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:めりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:馬に関する文化]]

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=draft}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=draft}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=draft}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育|12=draft}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=draft}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|12=draft}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=draft}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=draft}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=draft}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=draft}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=draft}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|12=draft}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=draft}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=draft}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year|12=draft}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=draft}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=draft}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=draft}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=draft}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=draft}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|12=draft}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=draft}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=draft}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=draft}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=draft}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|12=draft}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|12=draft}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=draft}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|12=draft}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|12=draft}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|12=draft}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|12=draft}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=draft}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=draft}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

=== Sources === {{Reflist}}

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft}}. == History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year|qq=draft}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==

revision.text:
馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/care|qq=}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court (Japan)|qq=}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabe|qq=}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/711|qq=}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Sanetomo|qq=}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rensho|qq=}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

revision.text:
馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/care|qq=}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court (Japan)|qq=}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabe|qq=}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/711|qq=}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Sanetomo|qq=}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rensho|qq=}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>

These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabe|qq=}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.

=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. === Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.

The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Sanetomo|qq=}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rensho|qq=}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/care|qq=}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court (Japan)|qq=}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabe|qq=}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/711|qq=}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Sanetomo|qq=}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rensho|qq=}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

<!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō-->

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|care|ja|飼育|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/care|qq=}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court (Japan)|qq=}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Mabe|ja|馬部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabe|qq=}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|711|ja|711年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/711|qq=}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|ja|765年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/765|qq=}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in {{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|808|ja|808年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/808|qq=}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|河内源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|ja|源実朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Sanetomo|qq=}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and {{ill|Rensho|ja|連署|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rensho|qq=}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|ja|足利義政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qq=}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|ja|里見氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi clan|qq=}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|ja|徳川慶喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qq=}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

[[Category:Horses in Japanese culture]] }}

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

[[sn:Meryō]] [[ja:馬寮]] [[en:Meryō]]
<!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--><!--Meryō--> [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:Horses in culture]]

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|qq=}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|qq=}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|qq=}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|qq=}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|qq=}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|qq=}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|qq=}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|qq=}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|qq=}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|qq=}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|qq=}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|qq=}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|qq=}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|qq=}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|qq=}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|qq=}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=執権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}} and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|qq=}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
