<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.

Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

{{wikidata link|Q11667945}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].

* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

{{wikidata link|Q11667945}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|馬寮}}

Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>

The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.

This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬寮]][[ko:마료]][[zh:馬寮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667945]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Tenkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the [[:en:Captivity (animal)|care]] and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=training|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]]. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called [[:en:Radical 187|Mabe]] oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Shiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in [[:en:AD 711|711]], when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Katsuragi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620959}} (later [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Meryōgen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In [[:en:765|765]], the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Naikyūryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Shumerō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} in [[:en:781|781]], with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Shume no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In [[:en:AD 808|808]], the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Hyōmashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the [[:en:Kawachi Genji|Minamoto clan]] and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === [[:en:Minamoto no Sanetomo|Minamoto no Sanetomo]], the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as [[:en:Shikken|Shikken]] and [[:en:Rensho|Rensho]] often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} heads, including [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimasa|Ashikaga Yoshimasa]], often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the [[:en:Satomi clan|Satomi clan]], self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshinobu|Tokugawa Yoshinobu]].
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
