It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|Captivity (animal)|en|Captivity (animal)|qid=Q2920296|lt=care|1=Captivity (animal)}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=Q8193822|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|qid=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Q11399928|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Radical 187|en|Radical 187|qid=Q294290|lt=Mabe|1=Radical 187}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Q60986811|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|AD 711|en|AD 711|qid=Q23519|lt=711|1=AD 711}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Q11620959|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620959}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|qid=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Q60985174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|en|765|qid=Q31216|lt=765|1=765}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Q60985176|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Q60985177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985177}} in {{ill|781|en|781|qid=Q31175|lt=781|1=781}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Q60985177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|AD 808|en|AD 808|qid=Q30366|lt=808|1=AD 808}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Q11393722|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|qid=Q5360652|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Kawachi Genji}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|en|Minamoto no Sanetomo|qid=Q174249|lt=Minamoto no Sanetomo|1=Minamoto no Sanetomo}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|qid=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}} and {{ill|Rensho|en|Rensho|qid=Q497960|lt=Rensho|1=Rensho}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qid=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28692536}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|en|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qid=Q447034|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimasa|1=Ashikaga Yoshimasa}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|qid=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan|1=Satomi clan}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|en|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qid=Q287657|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinobu|1=Tokugawa Yoshinobu}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants

馬寮
{{Multiple issues|Refimprove=June 2018}} '''Meryō''' (馬寮, also pronounced as Uma no Tsukasa) was one of the government offices under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. The Tang-derived name for this office was '''{{ill|Tenkyū|ja|典厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkyū|1=Q11393751|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393751}}'''. It was divided into two departments: the '''{{ill|Left Meryō|ja|左馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}''' (Sameryō) and the '''{{ill|Right Meryō|ja|右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Meryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}''' (Umeryō).<ref name=":0">{{WAP|pid=11863908|url=www.hidaka.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ts/tss/umabunka/08-yougo/04-kaisetsu-pop/meryou/index.html|title=Horse Culture Hidaka: Glossary Search: Meryō|date=October 27, 2021}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Meryō was responsible for the {{ill|Captivity (animal)|en|Captivity (animal)|qid=Q2920296|lt=care|1=Captivity (animal)}} and {{ill|training|ja|調教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/training|1=Q8193822|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193822}} of horses owned by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|qid=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}}. These horses were collected as tribute from provincial {{ill|official pastures|ja|御牧・官牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official pastures|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Horses were housed either in stables or pastures directly managed by the Meryō or in nearby provinces assigned to care for them. The Meryō also supervised {{ill|Imperial pastures|ja|勅旨牧|1=Q11399928|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial pastures|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11399928}} and supplied horses for military and ceremonial purposes.
Officials of the Meryō, classified as {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19598808}}, were permitted to carry swords. They also occasionally supported the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} in maintaining law and order in the capital. This connection to policing duties likely influenced the appointment of {{ill|samurai|ja|武士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|1=bushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1975935}} to Meryō positions in later years. Subordinates called {{ill|Radical 187|en|Radical 187|qid=Q294290|lt=Mabe|1=Radical 187}} oversaw the care of horses, supervising {{ill|Shiko|ja|飼戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiko|1=Q60986811|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60986811}}, who were directly responsible for their maintenance.
== History ==
=== Nara Period === Under the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, the Meryō was established with two divisions: the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. Initially headed by officers called {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}} and {{ill|Uma no Kami|ja|右馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uma no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}, the office gained importance in {{ill|AD 711|en|AD 711|qid=Q23519|lt=711|1=AD 711}}, when the prince {{ill|Katsuragi no Ō|ja|葛木王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Ō|1=Q11620959|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620959}} (later {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|qid=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}) was appointed as the {{ill|Meryōgen|ja|馬寮監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgen|1=Q60985174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985174}}, a newly created post to oversee both departments.
In {{ill|765|en|765|qid=Q31216|lt=765|1=765}}, the {{ill|Konoe|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoe|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} established the {{ill|Naikyūryō|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikyūryō|1=Q60985176|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985176}}, which took over the management of palace stables. This led to the integration of the Meryō into the {{ill|Shumerō|ja|主馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumerō|1=Q60985177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985177}} in {{ill|781|en|781|qid=Q31175|lt=781|1=781}}, with {{ill|Shume no Kami|ja|主馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shume no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主馬寮|1=Q60985177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985177}} as the new head.
=== Heian Period === In {{ill|AD 808|en|AD 808|qid=Q30366|lt=808|1=AD 808}}, the Shumerō was restructured into the Left Meryō and the Right Meryō. It absorbed the {{ill|Hyōmashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōmashi|1=Q11393722|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393722}}, and the ceremonial role of the Meryōgen was revived as {{ill|Meryōgogen|ja|馬寮御監|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meryōgogen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}. By the late Heian period, notable samurai from the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|qid=Q5360652|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Kawachi Genji}} and {{ill|Taira clan|ja|伊勢平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}} frequently held positions such as {{ill|Sama no Kami|ja|左馬頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 馬寮|1=Meryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667945}}, making the Meryō prestigious among the warrior class.
=== Kamakura Period === {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|en|Minamoto no Sanetomo|qid=Q174249|lt=Minamoto no Sanetomo|1=Minamoto no Sanetomo}}, the third Kamakura shōgun, held the title of Left Meryōgogen. Prominent figures such as {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|qid=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}} and {{ill|Rensho|en|Rensho|qid=Q497960|lt=Rensho|1=Rensho}} often served as Meryō officers.
=== Muromachi Period === {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qid=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} was appointed as the Right Meryōgogen. Subsequent {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 足利将軍家|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28692536}} heads, including {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|en|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qid=Q447034|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimasa|1=Ashikaga Yoshimasa}}, often held Meryō titles. Additionally, some regional clans, such as the {{ill|Satomi clan|en|Satomi clan|qid=Q2975367|lt=Satomi clan|1=Satomi clan}}, self-proclaimed the title of Sama no Kami.
=== Edo Period === The Meryō titles continued to hold ceremonial value under the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} was designated as the Left Meryōgogen, while {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} served as the Right Meryōgogen. The role of Right Meryōgogen remained a symbolic title for Tokugawa shoguns until the last, {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|en|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qid=Q287657|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinobu|1=Tokugawa Yoshinobu}}.
== Roles and Positions ==
* '''御監 (Meryōgogen):''' Ceremonial role; usually held by {{ill|Konoetai|ja|近衛大将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoetai|1=Konoe Daisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638972}} (Rank: {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}) * '''頭 (Kami):''' Administrative heads of Left and Right Meryō (Rank: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}) * '''助 (Suke):''' Assistant heads (Rank: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}) * '''允 (Jō):''' Subordinate officers (Ranks: {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|qq=}} and {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}}) * '''馬医 (Uma no I):''' Veterinarians (Rank: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}) * '''馬部 (Mabe):''' Horse attendants
